plant.description: Unique History: Sacredly known as 'Betel Nut'. Associated with Lord Vishnu and used in various religious ceremonies in India. Mentioned in ancient texts for its beauty and the 'sweetness of life' contrast with the 'pain of attachment'. Physical Characteristics: A tall, slender, unbranched palm tree reaching up to 20-30 meters in height, with a majestic crown and brownish-grey, rough bark. The flowers are small, white, and fragrant. The fruit is an oval-to-globose drupe (orange-red when ripe) containing a single, hard, brownish seed with a mottled white-brown interior. Found throughout South India and Southeast Asia and cultivated globably for fruit and medicine. Specific Chemical Compounds: Contains Arecoline, Catechuic acid, and Tannins. The presence of arecoline is crucial for its neuro-relaxant, anti-tumor, and anti-inflammatory action on the heart. Strict Standardizations: The seed is standardized in API based on its brownish-grey color and characteristic extremadamente astringent-bitter-sweet taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<3%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>15%).

ayurvedic_properties.dosha_karma: Powerful Kapha and Pitta Hara (pacifies Kapha and Pitta doshas). Due to its Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Sheeta (cooling) Virya, it is a premier herb for 'Sangrahi' (absorbent) and 'Mutra-virechaniya' (diuretic) therapies. It clears the cold and heavy qualities of Kapha through its systemic astringent property.

seed_powder.method: 1-3g of fine powder taken with water or buttermilk for managing diarrhea and hemorrhoids.

seed_decoction.method: 20g of dried seed boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for expelling intestinal worms. Take twice daily before meals.

dental_powder.method: Combined with alum and other herbs for daily tooth-cleaning.

external_decoction_wash.method: 20g of seeds boiled in 1 liter of water; used as a localized wash for chronic skin itching and ulcers.

dosage.children: Generally NOT recommended for children internally. Used topically as dental powder.
dosage.elderly: Ideal for age-related Vata issues and maintaining oral health. Dosage: 1g of powder daily as tooth-powder.

medicinal_uses: Expert Gastrointestinal and Diarrhea Care: Puga is the premier Ayurvedic remedy for all conditions of burning hemorrhoids and chronic diarrhea. Its 'Grahi' (absorbent) and 'Kashaya' (astringent) actions make it highly effective for strengthening the gut lining and binding the stool. WHO-aligned research confirms its potent anthelmintic and astringent effects.

Dental and Oral Care Specialist: Historically used as 'Danta-manjan' (tooth-powder). Its antimicrobial and astringent nature makes it unmatched for preventing dental caries and bleeding gums. The seed powder is used for cooling the oral cavity.
Anthelmintic and Parasite Control: Its 'Krimighna' (parasite-destroying) nature provided by alkaloids like arecoline helps in expelling tapeworms and roundworms from the digestive tract.

Urinary and Kidney Support: As a mild diuretic, it helps in clearing urinary tract infections and reducing burning sensations during micturition by providing systemic cooling through its 'Mutra-virechaniya' property.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Alkaloids (Arecoline, Arecaidine, Guvacine), Tannins (Catechuic acid), and Fats. Arecoline is the primary bioactive responsible for its nootropic and anthelmintic effects.

safety_profile.warning: GRAS Status: NOT generally recognized as safe for long-term habitual consumption. Safe for short-term therapeutic use under professional guidance.
safety_profile.medical_supervision: Use under the guidance of a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner.
contraindications: Chronic use (especially as betel quid) is associated with oral cancer and should be strictly avoided. Use for therapeutic short-term only. Avoid in pregnancy and extreme high-acidity conditions.
possible_side_effects: Habitual use leads to teeth staining, oral submucous fibrosis, and central nervous system stimulation.
drug_interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-hypertensive or anti-psychotic medications.
